# ODTIS glossary

Normative terms from [Terminology registry](../registry/terminology.yaml) @ `0.9.0-draft`. Authoritative definitions also appear in [section 2](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md).

<p class="odtis-hub-meta" markdown="1"><strong>18 terms</strong> | Regenerate: <code>python3 scripts/generate-site-indexes.py</code></p>

</div>

---

### Active LoA {#active-loa}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Single LoA currently assigned to a subject; exposed via assurance_level claim.

### Authentication Assurance Level (AAL) {#aal}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

NIST SP 800-63-3 authentication category; mapped from VenID LoA in operator policy.

### Digital Trust Infrastructure (DTI) {#dti}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Combined Layer 1 identity services and Layer 2 trust network operated under operator governance.

### DTI Operator (Operator) {#operator}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Entity operating declared ODTIS profiles, publishing operator policy, PKI, and audit obligations.

### Exchange Gateway {#exchange-gateway}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Sole mTLS entry for partner exchange traffic on a trust network node.

### Federation {#federation}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 6 - Federation](../spec/06-federation/SPEC.md)

Bilateral, non-transitive trust between two operators or networks.

### Federation Assurance Level (FAL) {#fal}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

NIST SP 800-63-3 federation category; documented for gateway exchange controls.

### Identity Assurance Level (IAL) {#ial}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

NIST SP 800-63-3 identity proofing category; mapped from VenID LoA in operator policy.

### Layer 1 (Identity Product) {#layer-1}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Citizen- and RP-facing identity lifecycle: registration, proofing, LoA, IdP, consent, verification API.

### Layer 2 (Trust Network) {#layer-2}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Institutional exchange: partners, catalog, gateway, PKI, grants, audit.

### Level of Assurance (LoA) {#loa}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Ordinal proof strength: low, medium, high, or national (national requires E-Registry).

### Operator policy {#operator-policy}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Published criteria for proofing, LoA, authentication, mappings, and retention; must not weaken ODTIS MUST.

### Partner {#partner}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Institution registered on trust network with identity distinct from subject identity.

### Proofing {#proofing}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Collection and validation of identity evidence before LoA assignment or upgrade.

### Relying Party (RP) {#relying-party}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Registered application or organization consuming identity assertions or verification results.

### Service grant {#service-grant}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Authorization binding caller partner, provider partner, and catalogued service.

### Subject {#subject}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 2 - Terminology and LoA](../spec/02-terminology-loa/SPEC.md)

Natural or legal person with stable platform identifier (subject_id) registered and managed by Core Identity.

### Trust Network {#trust-network}

**Status:** normative | **Section:** [Section 4 - Trust network](../spec/04-trust-network/SPEC.md)

Operators and partners exchanging artifacts via gateway and catalog semantics.

---

## LoA levels

Canonical claim values and profile bindings from `terminology.yaml`.

| Level | Claim | Name | Core Identity | Notes |
|-------|-------|------|---------------|-------|
| low | `low` | Contact verified | yes | - |
| medium | `medium` | Document verified | yes | - |
| high | `high` | Document and biometrics | yes | biometric required |
| national | `national` | Registry verified | no | biometric required, Extended: E-Registry |

See also: [Profile comparison](PROFILES.md), [Requirements registry guide](../registry/README.md).
